raspberry enable ssh via command

raspberry enable ssh via command


sudo systemctl enable ssh
sudo systemctl start ssh

Uncategorized

how to compare two list file use bash command line

how to compare two list file use bash command line


#this command  will echo f1.txt  line not contain in f2.txt, import thing : file must sort firt
comm -23 f1.txt f2.txt

Uncategorized

npm ERR! peerinvalid The package react@15.6.1 does not satisfy its siblings&#8217; peerDependencies requirements!

npm ERR! peerinvalid The package react@15.6.1 does not satisfy its siblings’ peerDependencies requirements!


Updated npm npm install npm -g
Cleared cache: npm cache clear
Removed node_modules rm -rf node_modules
Reinstalled node_modules, npm install.
Share
2017-12-29
Uncategorized

OSError: [Errno 1] Operation not permitted: &#8216;/tmp/pip-l_eBpW-uninstall/System/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.7/Extras/lib/python/numpy-1.8.0rc1-py2.7.egg-info&#8217;

OSError: [Errno 1] Operation not permitted: ‘/tmp/pip-l_eBpW-uninstall/System/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.7/Extras/lib/python/numpy-1.8.0rc1-py2.7.egg-info’


#osx pip install scikit-image  skip update numpy
sudo pip install  --ignore-installed six scikit-image

error: could not create ‘/System/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.7/include/python2.7/greenlet’: Operation not permitted


sudo pip install --user scikit-image
Share
2017-11-23
Uncategorized

This copy of the Install OS X Yosemite application can’t be verified. It may have been corrupted or tampered with during downloading

This copy of the Install OS X Yosemite application can’t be verified. It may have been corrupted or tampered with during downloading

installation file for Yosemite has had its digital certificate expire. Which means after a certain date it will no longer work. To work around this issue you open the Date & Time preferences pane, uncheck the box for “Set date & time automatically” & set the date back to just after the time Yosemite was initially released (October 16, 2014) then run the installer.

In the installation menu, choose Utilities / Terminal and type date 1020170014 then quit Terminal and the installer works.

Share
2017-11-16
Uncategorized

install webdav on raspberry pi

install webdav on raspberry pi


sudo apt-get  install  -y apache2 vim
sudo a2enmod dav
sudo a2enmod dav_fs
sudo mkdir -p /var/www/webdav

sudo vim /etc/apache2/sites-available/000-default.conf

Alias /webdav  /var/www/webdav

<Location /webdav>
 Options Indexes
 DAV On
 AuthType Basic
 AuthName "webdav"
 AuthUserFile /etc/apache2/webdav.password
 Require valid-user
 </Location>

 sudo htpasswd -c /etc/apache2/webdav.password pi
 sudo chown root:www-data /etc/apache2/webdav.password
 sudo chmod 640 /etc/apache2/webdav.password

 sudo chown -R www-data:www-data  /var/www/webdav

sudo systemctl restart apache2
Share

arp-scan usage

arp-scan usage


#scan
arp-scan --interface=eth1 -l
#update oui
get-oui -v
#update iab
get-iab -v

2017-09-22
Uncategorized

How do you use posix_spawn to replace the deprecated &#8216;system&#8217; to launch opendiff in Objective-C?

How do you use posix_spawn to replace the deprecated ‘system’ to launch opendiff in Objective-C?

Using posix_spawn(), to answer your question:


#include <spawn.h>
extern char **environ;
pid_t pid;
char *argv[] = {
    "/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/usr/bin/opendiff",
    "/Users/LukeSkywalker/Documents/doc1.rtf",
    "/Users/LukeSkywalker/Documents/doc2.rtf",
    NULL
};

posix_spawn(&pid, argv[0], NULL, NULL, argv, environ);
waitpid(pid, NULL, 0);

Or, you could use NSTask:


NSTask *task = [[NSTask alloc] init];
task.launchPath = @"/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/usr/bin/opendiff";
task.arguments = [NSArray arrayWithObjects:
                  @"/Users/LukeSkywalker/Documents/doc1.rtf",
                  @"/Users/LukeSkywalker/Documents/doc2.rtf",
                  nil];
[task launch];
[task waitUntilExit];
Share
2017-09-15
Uncategorized

apt-get action via http proxy

apt-get action via http proxy


sudo apt-get -o Acquire::http::proxy="http://127.0.0.1:8118" update
sudo apt-get -o Acquire::socks::proxy="socks://127.0.0.1:7070" update
